data.title: JPMorgan's CEO Jamie Dimon criticized Bitcoin as 'ponzi' in 2025, yet JPMorgan launched a $100 million Ethereum fund amidst his skepticism.
data.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. CEO Jamie Dimon continued to express skepticism towards Bitcoin in 2025, despite the company launching a $100 million Ethereum fund. Dimon, known as a prominent crypto skeptic, made various remarks throughout the year regarding Bitcoin, claiming it has no inherent value and is associated with illicit activities like sex trafficking and money laundering. He even likened owning Bitcoin to smoking, stating that while JPMorgan clients can invest in digital assets, the bank will not offer custody services for them. In contrast to Dimon's personal views, JPMorgan has been actively building its crypto infrastructure.

data.title: Gold and silver hit record highs, with gold jumping 2.3% to $4,440/ounce and silver rallying 2.1% nearing $70/ounce. Gold YTD up 67%, silver up 133%. Midday trading sees Russell 2000 up 1.6%, S&P 500 up 0.8% to 6,875, Nasdaq 100 up 0.5%.
data.description: Precious metals, specifically gold and silver, are continuing to steal the spotlight at the beginning of Christmas week, with both hitting fresh record highs. Gold saw a significant jump of 2.3% to reach $4,440 per ounce, its most robust daily increase in over a month. Year-to-date, gold has surged by 67%. Silver also experienced a rally, increasing by 2.1% and edging closer to the key $70 mark. So far this year, silver has shown impressive growth of 133%. The VanEck Gold Miners ETF and the Global X Silver Miners ETF, tracking shares of gold and silver miners, surged by 3.6% and 5% respectively. In midday trading in New York, the Russell 2000 climbed by 1.6%, the S&P 500 rose by 0.8% to 6,875, trading just below record highs by less than 1%. The Nasdaq 100, which is tech-focused, also saw an increase of 0.5%. This positive momentum in the market comes as all major U.S. equity benchmarks are extending gains for a third consecutive session, entering a traditionally strong period in the calendar.

data.title: Palantir (PLTR) stock thrives in 2025 with strong earnings beats, revenue at $1.18 billion (62.8% YoY increase), earning a spot on the Nice list for 2026.
data.description: Palantir Technologies Inc, listed on NASDAQ under the symbol PLTR, has been impressing investors in 2025 with its standout performance. The company consistently exceeded earnings expectations throughout the year, solidifying its position on the Nice list as we head into 2026. The stock of Palantir Technologies is on an upward trend. In November, the Denver-based AI and data analytics firm delivered one of the season's strongest financial reports, surpassing earnings estimates by 25% with earnings per share (EPS) of 21 cents compared to the forecasted 17 cents. The company reported revenue of $1.18 billion, marking a significant 62.8% increase from the previous year. Additionally, Palantir raised its guidance for future performance. For further details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: FDA approved Novo Nordisk A/S' Wegovy pill (25mg oral semaglutide) for weight loss. 16.6% mean weight loss in trial.
data.description: The FDA recently approved Novo Nordisk A/S' Wegovy pill, containing 25 mg of oral semaglutide, as a weight management treatment. This marks the first approval of an oral glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonist for weight management. Clinical trials such as OASIS 4 showed that the pill led to a 16.6% mean weight loss in adults with obesity or overweight with comorbidities. The pill's efficacy was comparable to the injectable version of Wegovy at 2.4 mg, with one in three participants experiencing a 20% or greater weight reduction. Novo Nordisk plans to launch the Wegovy pill soon.

data.title: Stocks surge with Dow up 0.48% to 48,673.55, NASDAQ up 0.09% to 23,582.81, S&P 500 up 0.25% to 6,927.29. Jobless claims decline to 214,000. Edgewise Therapeutics Inc shares rise 21% to $26.36.
data.description: Stocks in the U.S. saw a boost in midday trading, as the Dow Jones index surged over 200 points on Wednesday, reaching 48,673.55, up by 0.48%. The NASDAQ also rose by 0.09% to 23,582.81, while the S&P 500 climbed 0.25% to 6,927.29. Market sentiment improved, with the S&P 500 hitting a record close and the fear index remaining in the 'greed' zone. Consumer staples shares increased by 0.7%, but communication services stocks fell by 0.1%. Initial jobless claims in the U.S. dropped by 10,000 to 214,000 in the week ending Dec. 20, beating market estimates of 223,000. Edgewise Therapeutics Inc (NASDAQ: EWTX) saw its shares surge by 21% to $26.36 on Wednesday after announcing updates from the ongoing CIRRUS-HCM Phase.

data.title: FCC bans foreign drones, boosting US industry. DJI, Autel blocked as US market vacuums. DJI had 70-80% market share. Domestic stocks surge.
data.description: This week, the U.S. drone industry received a significant boost as the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) made a national security determination and placed foreign-made uncrewed aircraft systems (UAS) and their critical components on the "Covered List," effectively banning Chinese drone makers DJI and Autel, along with other foreign manufacturers, from receiving FCC authorizations for sale in the U.S. This move has created a vacuum in the market where DJI previously held a market share of nearly 70-80%. While existing drone fleets are still operational, the inability to import or sell new hardware has impacted the industry. The banning of foreign-made drones has caused domestic drone stocks to surge, with RCAT stock showing significant movement. Investors are closely monitoring the situation and its impact on the industry.

data.title: Stock Market update: Nvidia stock rises, Dow lags. Small caps down, Quantum Name sold-off after rally.
data.description: In today's stock market, small-cap stocks were the notable underperformers, while Nvidia showed strength in Friday afternoon trading. The Dow Jones Industrial Average lagged behind. A quantum company experienced a sell-off following a previous rally. This news was reported live on Investor's Business Daily, where updates on market performance and individual stock movements were provided. The focus was on Nvidia's climb and the weakness in small-cap stocks, with specific mention of the Dow's performance relative to these trends.

data.description: On December 26, 2025, Sidus Space, Inc. disclosed the pricing details of their best-efforts offering. The company, listed as SIDU on Nasdaq, released 10,800,000 shares of its Class A common stock to the market. Sidus, a pioneer in space and defense technology, is making this move to raise funds and potentially expand its operations. This latest development marks a significant step for the company in the realm of investment and growth.

data.title: Alphabet Inc. leads tech giants with 66% gains in 2025. 8 ETFs from abroad surpass it, with iShares MSCI Greece ETF up 73%.
data.description: Alphabet Inc's parent company Google's shares (NASDAQ: GOOG, GOOGL) have seen a 66% increase year-to-date, outperforming other mega-cap tech giants like Nvidia Corp (NASDAQ: NVDA) at 42%. However, the real surprise lies in global markets, with eight country ETFs delivering stronger returns than Alphabet. These include iShares MSCI South Africa ETF (EZA) up 67.3%, iShares MSCI Austria ETF (EWO) up 67.9%, Global X – MSCI Colombia ETF (COLO) up 68.5%, iShares MSCI Poland ETF (EPOL) up 68.6%, and iShares MSCI Spain ETF (EWP) up 73%. The story is available on Benzinga.com for more details on global investment performance beyond Big Tech.
